required: All conclusions are tied to the current head SHA.
Do not inspect source files, diffs, tests, git history, implementation notes, or review bundles.
If implementation material becomes visible, mark the run contaminated and restart with a fresh validator.
Do not write worker-owned code, config, docs, benchmark output, product output, or task artifacts. That work belongs to the worker or a fresh worker.
The controller owns the stable parent outcome, required assertions, amendment history, assignments, routing, evidence checks, and completion judgment.
Name one writer for each mutable control surface.
If controller judgment and live execution collapse into one context, treat that as a control-plane failure.
The ledger records authority, current state, evidence, and recovery gates.
Changing the field does not grant permission.
The controller owns the parent outcome, assertion text, amendments, and final completion judgment.
After compaction, restart, or handoff, reopen authoritative inputs in precedence order.
Do not launch another symptom-level successor when the failure signature, inputs, and runtime path are materially unchanged and the prior attempt produced no new discriminating evidence.
Name one writer for benchmark policy and queue ownership.
The launchers fail open if the guard script or Python runner is missing. The portable repo checks catch that state before hook definitions are treated as accepted.
